highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.214 Released
highlights.summary: This release patches a critical security bug where `dir/**` allow rules like `Edit(src/**)` could accidentally auto-approve writes to nested directories anywhere in the file tree instead of only within your current working directory. It also fixes a permission-check bypass on Windows PowerShell 5.1, making Claude Code safer to use across platforms. If you use glob-based permission rules, updating immediately is recommended.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.212 Released
highlights.summary: The `/fork` command has been redesigned — it now copies your conversation into a new background session visible in `claude agents`, so you can keep working in your current session without interruption. The old in-session subagent behavior is now available via the `/subtask` command. A new `claude auto-mode reset` command also lets you restore default auto-mode settings quickly.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.210 Released
highlights.summary: Long-running tool calls now show a live elapsed-time counter so you can see progress instead of wondering if Claude is stuck. This release also adds startup warnings if you use deprecated permission rules like `Write(path)` or `Glob(path)` — you should migrate to `Edit(path)` or `Read(path)` to avoid future breakage.

highlights.title: Claude Code sends 33k tokens before reading the prompt; OpenCode sends 7k
highlights.summary: A developer team noticed their token usage was significantly higher with Claude Code than OpenCode and investigated the overhead. They found Claude Code consumes roughly 33,000 tokens in context setup before it even processes your actual prompt, compared to around 7,000 for OpenCode — a nearly 5x difference that adds up quickly if you're on a metered plan. Understanding this overhead can help you budget API costs and decide when to use lighter-weight alternatives.

highlights.title: Show HN: OtoDock – Run Claude Code and Codex as a Team of Agents on Your Server
highlights.summary: OtoDock is an open-source tool that lets you run Claude Code and OpenAI Codex together as a collaborative team of agents on your own homelab or business server. Instead of running agents from your local terminal, you can deploy and manage them centrally across virtual machines. This is a great option for developers who want to offload long-running agentic tasks to a server without keeping their laptop open.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a focused 30-minute walkthrough aimed at getting developers productive with Claude Code quickly. It's a good middle-ground option if you want more depth than a quick-start guide but don't have hours to spare on a full course. Ideal for developers who have already installed Claude Code and want to learn best practices efficiently.

highlights.title: CLAUDE CODE FULL COURSE 4 HOURS: Build & Sell (2026)
highlights.summary: A comprehensive 4-hour video course covering Claude Code from setup all the way through building and shipping real projects in 2026. This is one of the most in-depth free resources available if you want to go from zero to confidently using Claude Code for professional work. Best suited for learners who prefer structured, hands-on video instruction.

highlights.title: Claude Code: Anatomy of a Misfeature
highlights.summary: This opinion piece dissects a specific Claude Code behavior that the author argues is a design flaw rather than a feature, exploring how it can lead to unexpected results in real workflows. It's a thoughtful critical read that can help developers understand the tool's limitations and set more realistic expectations. Beginner users especially benefit from knowing where Claude Code's behavior may surprise them.

tips_of_the_day: Audit your Claude Code permission rules today: if you're using `Write(path)` or `Glob(path)` in your settings, replace them with `Edit(path)` or `Read(path)` — the old rules are deprecated and may behave unexpectedly in future releases.

Use `/fork` to spin off a parallel background session when you want to explore a risky refactor without losing your current conversation — check `claude agents` to switch between sessions, and use `/subtask` for quick in-session delegations instead.

Keep an eye on your token consumption if you're on a metered Claude API plan: Claude Code's context overhead is high by design, so batch related tasks into a single session rather than starting fresh sessions for every small question to get more value per token.
